customSqlDownload.help.whatIsThis:
This download format allows you to aggregate occurrences by their taxonomic, temporal and/or spatial properties. For example, a data cube can be configured to aggregate occurrences by family, month and grid cell of the European Environment Agency reference grid (three dimensions) and count the number of occurrences (a measure) per combination. The result is a CSV file.

Once configured, a SQL query will be created to generate the data cube. For more advanced use, it is possible to further customize the requested download by editing the SQL query.
customSqlDownload.help.dimensions: A dimension represents an aspect along which data can be aggregated. Selecting a higher resolution (e.g., species over family, date over year, 100 m over 10 km) will result in more categories and therefore more records.
customSqlDownload.help.taxonomicDimensio: This dimension aggregates occurrences by their taxonomic rank.
customSqlDownload.help.temporalDimension: This dimension aggregates occurrences by time.
customSqlDownload.help.dataQuality: Additional quality filters to apply to the data.
customSqlDownload.help.grid: This dimension aggregates occurrences in a spatial grid.
customSqlDownload.help.gridResolution: The size of each grid cell.
customSqlDownload.help.randomizePoints: For occurrence records with a coordinate uncertainty that covers more than one grid cell, should a random cell be chosen? If not, the cell containing the centroid of the record is used.
customSqlDownload.help.measurements: A calculated quantitative value for each combination of dimensions.
customSqlDownload.help.higherTaxonomy: Additional higher taxonomic ranks for which the number of occurrences should also be included. Useful to assert sampling bias.
customSqlDownload.help.minCoordinateUnce: The lowest recorded coordinate uncertainty (in meters). Useful to assert the spatial precision of the data.
customSqlDownload.help.minTemporalUncert: The lowest recorded temporal uncertainty (in seconds). Useful to assert the temporal precision of the data.
customSqlDownload.help.editSql: The easiest way to download and explore data is via the occurrence search user interface. But for complex queries and aggregations, the SQL editor provides more freedom.
trends.theseChartsIllustrate1: These charts illustrate the change in availability of the species occurrence records over time.
trends.occByKingdomChartText: The number of available records categorized by kingdom. "Unknown" includes records with taxonomic information that cannot be linked to available taxonomic checklists.
trends.animaliaByBasisOfRecordText: The number of animal records categorized by the basis of record. "Unknown" includes records without defined basis of record or with an unrecognized value for basis of record.
trends.plantaeByBasisOfRecordText: The number of plant records categorized by the basis of record. "Unknown" includes records without defined basis of record or with an unrecognized value for basis of record.
trends.theseChartsIllustrate2: These charts illustrate the change in the number of species for which occurrence records are available.
trends.speciesCountDescription: Species counts are based on the number of binomial scientific names for which GBIF has received data records, organized as far as possible using synonyms recorded in key databases such as the Catalogue of Life. Since many names are not yet included in these databases, some proportion of these names will be unrecognized synonyms and do not represent valid species. Therefore these counts can be used as an indication of richness only, and do not represent true species counts. All data have been processed using the same, most recent, version of the common GBIF backbone taxonomy, and comparisons over time are therefore realistic.
trends.numOccSpeciesByKingdom: The number of species with available occurrence records, categorized by kingdom.
trends.numSpeciesFromSpecimens: The number of species associated with specimen records.
trends.numSpeciesAssociatedWithObsRecord: The number of species associated with observation records.
trends.theseChartsIllustrate3: These charts illustrate changes in the spread of records by year of occurrence and by day of year, indicating the extent of possible bias towards more recent periods or particular seasons. Snapshots are provided for approximately 3-year intervals to show changes in spread.
trends.speciesCountsAreBasedOnTheNumberO: Species counts are based on the number of binomial scientific names for which GBIF has received data records, organized as far as possible using synonyms recorded in key databases such as the Catalogue of Life. Since many names are not yet included in these databases, some proportion of these names will be unrecognized synonyms and do not represent valid species. Therefore these counts can be used as an indication of richness only, and do not represent true species counts. All data have been processed using the same, most recent, version of the common GBIF backbone taxonomy, and comparisons over time are therefore realistic.
trends.theNumberOfOccurrenceRecordsAvail: The number of occurrence records available for each year since 1950.
trends.theNumberOfSpeciesForWhichRecords: The number of species (see above) for which records are available for each year since 1950.
trends.recordsAvailableForEachDayOfTheYe: The number of occurrence records available for each day of the year.
trends.speciesForWhichRecordsAreAvailabl: The number of species (see above) for which records are available for each day of the year.
trends.theseChartsMayRevealPatternsThatR: These charts may reveal patterns that represent biases in data collection (seasonality, public holidays) or potential issues in data management (disproportionate numbers of records shown for the first or last days in the year or each month or week). Such issues may arise at various stages in data processing and require further investigation.
trends.byGeneratingTheseChartsAnIssueWas: By generating these charts [an issue](https://github.com/gbif/gbif-api/issues/2) was detected in the GBIF processing which contributes to the spike seen on the first day of the year in several charts and will be addressed.
trends.theseChartsIllustrate4: These charts illustrate changes in the completeness (see below) of available records and in the precision of these records with respect to time, geography and taxonomy.
trends.aRecordIsHereDefinedToBeCompleteI: A record is here defined to be complete if it includes an identification at least to species rank, valid coordinates, a full date of occurrence and a given basis of record (e.g. Observation, specimen etc).
trends.theseChartsIllustrate5: These charts illustrate changes in the number of records considered complete according to the definition above. Separate charts separately show the same information for specimen records and for observation records. Subsequent charts illustrate the component elements that affect the number of complete records.
trends.theseChartsIllustrate6: These charts illustrate changes in the number of available records which include an identification at least to the species rank. The numbers of records identified to an infraspecific rank or to a genus are also shown.
trends.theseChartsIllustrate7: These charts illustrate changes in the number of available records which include coordinates for which no known issues have been detected. For records without accepted valid coordinates, these charts also show the number of records for which the country of occurrence is known.
trends.theseChartsIllustrate8: These charts illustrate changes in the number of available records which include a complete date including year, month and day. The numbers of records including only the month and year or only the year are also shown.
trends.theseChartsIllustrate9: These charts illustrate change in the number of species for which occurrence records are available and the geographic coverage of records for each species at different scales. In each chart, species are classified by the number of cells at a given scale for which occurrence records for the species are available. More cells indicates greater data coverage for the species. Naturally, some species are only known from a small area, so low cell counts do not necessarily indicate lack of relevant data.

directory.title: Directory
directory.group.executiveCommittee.title: Directiecomité
directory.group.executiveCommittee.descr: The Executive Committee operates on behalf of the Governing Board when the Board is not in session and within the areas of responsibility delegated to it by the Governing Board. Among its functions are monitoring of the performance of the Secretariat in carrying out the decisions taken by the Governing Board including implementation of the Strategic Plan and the Work Programme, and management of the budget.
directory.group.scienceCommittee.title: Wetenschapscommissie
directory.group.scienceCommittee.descrip: The GBIF Science Committee is an advisory committee that oversees the development and progress of the GBIF work programme and makes recommendations to the Governing Board, the Executive Committee and the Secretariat. You can read its terms of reference [here.](/document/80877/current-terms-of-reference-for-the-science-committee)
directory.group.budgetCommittee.title: Begrotingscommissie
directory.group.budgetCommittee.descript: The Budget Committee is an advisory committee dealing with various financial issues relating to the administration of GBIF funds. Among other functions, it oversees the audit of the annual accounts submitted to the Governing Board, and it provides guidance for the selection of GBIF’s auditing company. The committee makes recommendations to the Governing Board, the Executive Committee and the Secretariat. Its terms of reference can be found [here.](/document/80669/terms-of-reference-for-the-gbif-budget-committee)
directory.group.nsg.title: Nodes Steering Group
directory.group.nsg.description: Established in 2011, the Nodes Steering Group (NSG) consists of the chair and vice-chairs of the [Participant Node Managers Committee](/contact-us/directory?group=nodesCommittee) joined by a regional representative from each of GBIF’s six regions ([Africa](/the-gbif-network/africa), [Asia](/the-gbif-network/asia), [Europe and Central Asia](/the-gbif-network/europe), [Latin America/Caribbean](/the-gbif-network/latin-america), [North America](/the-gbif-network/north-america) and [Oceania](/the-gbif-network/oceania)). The NSG’s objectives are to formulate specific recommendations to relevant GBIF bodies based on feedback provided by Participant nodes, and to provide advice on GBIF’s annual work programmes as they relate to the efforts of GBIF nodes.
directory.group.nodesCommittee.title: Node Managers Committee
directory.group.nodesCommittee.descripti: The Participant Node Managers Committee (or Nodes Committee) is a dynamic group formed by the node managers appointed by all Participants (countries and international organizations). The committee includes three Chair positions established through voting by the Governing Board for a maximum of two two-year terms. You can read its terms of reference [here](/document/80571/terms-of-reference-for-the-participant-node-managers-committee-nodes).
directory.group.secretariat.title: GBIF secretariaat
directory.group.secretariat.description:
The GBIF Secretariat, located at the Natural History Museum in Copenhagen, Denmark, is charged with developing, executing and reporting on the GBIF work programme. The tasks of the Secretariat include:
 - operating the GBIF portal and associated informatics
 - coordinating activities of the network related to data mobilization, publishing and management
 - monitoring data gaps and use of GBIF-mediated data for science, and
 - operating central communication and collaboration services

This may be appropriate in situations where there are concerns of having GBIF host datasets. A tool may be converted from conversion-only mode to publishing mode later.

enums.issueHelp.NAME_PARENT_MISMATCH: The (accepted) bi/trinomial name does not match the parent name and should be recombined into the parent genus/species. For example the species Picea alba with a parent genus Abies is a mismatch and should be replaced by Abies alba. GBIF backbone specific issue.
enums.issueHelp.DWCA_UNREADABLE: Onmogelijk om de DarwinCore Archief te lezen of extracten.
enums.issueHelp.RESOURCE_CREATOR_MISSING: The resource creator is missing or is incomplete
enums.issueHelp.PUBLISHED_BEFORE_GENUS: A bi/trinomial name published earlier than the parent genus was published. This might indicate that the name should rather be a recombination.
enums.issueHelp.ACCEPTED_NAME_USAGE_ID_I: The value for dwc:acceptedNameUsageID could not be resolved
enums.issueHelp.CONFLICTING_BASIONYM_COM: There have been more than one accepted name in a homotypical basionym group of names. GBIF backbone specific issue.
enums.issueHelp.INDIVIDUAL_COUNT_INVALID: Individual count value not parsable into an integer.
enums.issueHelp.RECORD_REFERENTIAL_INTEG: The record in an extension does not link to an existing core record
enums.issueHelp.COLUMN_MISMATCH: The number of column used on the line does not match the expected number
enums.issueHelp.MULTIMEDIA_URI_INVALID: An invalid uri is given for a multimedia object.
enums.issueHelp.LICENSE_MISSING_OR_UNKNO: The licence can not be parsed, is not supported by GBIF or is simply missing
enums.issueHelp.PARENT_CYCLE: The child parent classification resulted into a cycle that needed to be resolved/cut.
enums.issueHelp.MODIFIED_DATE_INVALID: A (partial) invalid date is given for dc:modified, such as a non existing date, invalid zero month, etc.
enums.issueHelp.REQUIRED_TERM_MISSING: A required term for the core/extension is missing
enums.issueHelp.GEODETIC_DATUM_ASSUMED_W: Indicating that the interpreted coordinates assume they are based on WGS84 datum as the datum was either not indicated or interpretable. See  GEODETIC_DATUM_INVALID.
enums.issueHelp.COUNTRY_MISMATCH: Interpreted country for dwc:country and dwc:countryCode contradict each other.
enums.issueHelp.UNKNOWN_ROWTYPE: The rowType used for the core/extension is unknown
enums.issueHelp.BIB_REFERENCE_INVALID: At least one bibliographic reference extension record attached to this name usage is invalid.
enums.issueHelp.CHAINED_SYNOYM: If a synonym points to another synonym as its accepted taxon the chain is resolved
enums.issueHelp.ELEVATION_MIN_MAX_SWAPPE: Set if supplied min > max elevation
enums.issueHelp.MULTIMEDIA_DATE_INVALID: An invalid date is given for dc:created of a multimedia object.
enums.issueHelp.COORDINATE_REPROJECTION_: Indicates successful coordinate reprojection according to provided datum, but which results in a datum shift larger than 0.1 decimal degrees.
enums.issueHelp.BASIONYM_AUTHOR_MISMATCH: The authorship of the original name does not match the authorship in brackets of the actual name.
enums.issueHelp.COUNTRY_DERIVED_FROM_COO: The interpreted country is based on the coordinates, not the verbatim string information.
enums.issueHelp.RECORD_NOT_UNIQUELY_IDEN: The record in the core file is not uniquely identified
enums.issueHelp.ELEVATION_NOT_METRIC: Set if supplied elevation is not given in the metric system, for example using feet instead of metres
enums.issueHelp.PRESUMED_NEGATED_LONGITU: Longitude appears to be negated, e.g. 32.3 instead of -32.3
enums.issueHelp.RECORD_IDENTIFIER_NOT_FO: No record identifier was found within the file
enums.issueHelp.DWCA_META_XML_SCHEMA: Meta.xml file can not be validated against its schema
enums.issueHelp.COORDINATE_REPROJECTED: The original coordinate was successfully reprojected from a different geodetic datum to WGS84.
enums.issueHelp.RECORDED_DATE_MISMATCH: The date represented by eventDate and the atomic version (year, month, day) does not represent the same date. Warning: https://github.com/gbif/parsers/issues/8
enums.issueHelp.DWCA_META_XML_NOT_FOUND: Onmogelijk om de meta.xml bestand te vinden.
enums.issueHelp.DESCRIPTION_MISSING_OR_T: The description of the dataset is missing or too short
enums.issueHelp.SCIENTIFIC_NAME_ASSEMBLE: The scientific name was assembled from the individual name parts and not given as a whole string
enums.issueHelp.DEPTH_NON_NUMERIC: Set if depth is a non numeric value
enums.issueHelp.INTERPRETATION_ERROR: An error occurred during interpretation, leaving the record interpretation incomplete.
enums.issueHelp.BACKBONE_MATCH_NONE: Name usage could not be matched to the GBIF backbone.
enums.issueHelp.EML_GBIF_SCHEMA: The EML document does not validate against the schema
enums.issueHelp.CONTINENT_COUNTRY_MISMAT: The interpreted continent and country do not match up.
enums.issueHelp.RELATIONSHIP_MISSING: There were problems representing all name usage relationships, i.e. the link to the parent, accepted and/or original name. The interpreted record in ChecklistBank is lacking some of the original source relation.
enums.issueHelp.COORDINATE_PRECISION_INV: Indicates an invalid or very unlikely coordinatePrecision
enums.issueHelp.HOMONYM: A not synonymized homonym exists for this name in some other backbone source which have been ignored at build time.
enums.issueHelp.ORTHOGRAPHIC_VARIANT: A potential orthographic variant exists in the backbone. GBIF backbone specific issue.
enums.issueHelp.VERNACULAR_NAME_INVALID: At least one vernacular name extension record attached to this name usage is invalid.
enums.issueHelp.DESCRIPTION_INVALID: At least one description extension record attached to this name usage is invalid.
enums.issueHelp.REFERENCES_URI_INVALID: An invalid uri is given for dc:references.
